name: Sherlock
description: Hunt down social media accounts by username across 400+ social networks.
bestFor: Enumerating a target's accounts from a single known username/handle.
trustNote: 85k-star flagship username OSINT tool, widely used and actively maintained.
notes: Pip-installable or via Docker. Outputs found profile URLs. Some false positives on sites with permissive 404s; verify hits manually. Great first step when you have a handle a missing person used.

name: Maigret
description: Collect a dossier on a person by username from 3000+ sites with reporting.
bestFor: Building a full username dossier with extracted profile metadata and PDF/HTML reports.
trustNote: Maintained by soxoj (OSINT trainer); successor concept to Sherlock with richer parsing.
notes: Parses page content to extract names, locations, links beyond mere presence. Generates HTML/PDF/JSON/XMind reports. Larger site list than Sherlock but slower; supports tag/country filtering.

name: Social Analyzer
description: API, CLI and Web App for analyzing and finding a person's profile across 1000+ social media sites.
bestFor: Profile discovery with string-analysis/ranking to reduce false positives.
trustNote: 23k-star tool by qeeqbox, offers profile-ranking and NSFW/metadata detection.
notes: Has CLI, REST API and a web UI. Uses multiple detection methods (string matching, top-N matching, metadata) and can rate match confidence. Useful when searching by full name as well as username.

name: PhoneInfoga
description: Information gathering framework for phone numbers.
bestFor: Identifying carrier, line type, country/region and footprint of an international phone number.
trustNote: 16k-star standard phone-OSINT framework with active maintenance.
notes: Validates and formats numbers, identifies carrier/line type, and runs scanner plugins (some require free API keys like Numverify/Google CSE). Has CLI, REST API and web client. Does not directly reveal owner identity but seeds further searches.

name: Seeker
description: Accurately locate smartphones using social engineering (geolocation via crafted phishing page).
bestFor: Obtaining precise GPS coordinates and device info from a cooperating/target device click.
trustNote: Popular but it is an active social-engineering/phishing tool; use only with authorization.
notes: Hosts a fake page (e.g. nearby restaurant) that requests location permission; captures GPS, device, OS, IP. Active and intrusive - legally sensitive. For missing persons, only relevant if a subject can be induced to click a link, e.g. with law-enforcement involvement.

name: theHarvester
description: Gathers emails, subdomains, hosts, employee names and more from public sources.
bestFor: Mapping the public footprint (emails, names, subdomains) tied to a domain or org.
trustNote: Long-standing 16k-star OSINT staple maintained by laramies.
notes: Queries many engines/datasets (some need free API keys). Most useful for org-linked email/name discovery rather than locating an individual person directly.

name: r4ven
description: Tracks GPS location and captures device/IP information via a hosted link.
bestFor: Capturing precise geolocation and device fingerprint when a target opens a link.
trustNote: Active social-engineering geolocation tool; authorization required.
notes: Similar to Seeker/ISeeYou: serves a page that requests location and logs device/IP/battery details to a dashboard. Intrusive and active.

name: I-See-You (ISeeYou)
description: Bash/JS tool to find a user's exact location during social-engineering or phishing.
bestFor: Pinpointing a target's location via a crafted link in an authorized engagement.
trustNote: Active phishing-style geolocation tool; legally sensitive.
notes: Uses HTML5 Geolocation API behind a public link via Serveo/ngrok. Same caveats as Seeker - active, intrusive, requires authorization.

name: reconspider
description: OSINT framework for scanning IP addresses, emails, websites, and organizations.
bestFor: Multi-target OSINT lookups (IP, email, phone, domain, username) from one console.
trustNote: Community OSINT framework; some modules depend on third-party APIs.
notes: Aggregates lookups for IPs, domains, emails, phone numbers and usernames. Mixed maintenance; verify modules still work against live APIs.

name: FBI-tools
description: Curated collection of OSINT tools for gathering information and digital forensics.
bestFor: Discovering additional OSINT/forensics tools organized by use case.
trustNote: Awesome-style aggregator list, not a single tool.
notes: A README directory of OSINT and forensics tools; useful as a frontier/index to mine for more people-search tooling.

name: sn0int
description: Semi-automatic OSINT framework and package manager for scripted recon modules.
bestFor: Running and chaining community OSINT modules (domains, emails, geo, social) with a local datastore.
trustNote: Well-regarded Rust OSINT framework by kpcyrd with a module registry.
notes: Stores entities in a graph-like local DB; modules can geolocate wifi/IP, enumerate subdomains, query social. Scriptable in Lua. Powerful but has a learning curve.

name: Maryam
description: Modular open-source intelligence (OSINT) framework with search-engine and meta modules.
bestFor: Open-source footprinting via search engines, DNS, emails and social meta-search.
trustNote: Recon-ng-style modular OSINT framework, community maintained.
notes: Inspired by recon-ng/theHarvester. Has search, DNS, social and document modules. Useful for broad footprinting around a name or domain.

name: metabigor
description: OSINT tools for intelligence gathering without requiring API keys.
bestFor: No-API-key recon on IP ranges, ASN, organizations and certificates.
trustNote: By j3ssie (Osmedeus author); focuses on infra recon.
notes: Infrastructure-focused (IP/ASN/org/cert recon). Low direct relevance to locating a person.

name: DataSurgeon
description: Quickly extracts emails, IPs, hashes, credit cards and other selectors from text.
bestFor: Pulling structured selectors (emails, phones, IPs) out of dumps, logs or scraped text.
trustNote: Lightweight Rust extraction utility, community maintained.
notes: Reads piped text/files and extracts emails, phone numbers, IPs, MACs, hashes, credit cards, URLs. Handy for triaging breach dumps or scraped pages tied to a person.

name: OSINT-Cheat-sheet
description: OSINT cheat sheet listing tools, wikis, datasets, articles and books.
bestFor: Reference index of OSINT techniques, tools and datasets across categories.
trustNote: Curated cheat-sheet/awesome list, not an executable tool.
notes: Index of OSINT resources by category (people, social, geo, etc.). Useful frontier to mine additional people-search tooling and datasets.

name: Argus
description: All-in-one information gathering toolkit with many modular recon scripts.
bestFor: Running a broad battery of domain/network/intel recon modules from one CLI.
trustNote: Popular aggregate recon toolkit; mostly network/domain focused.
notes: Bundles dozens of modules (DNS, WHOIS, geo-IP, ports, etc.). Primarily infrastructure recon; limited direct person-finding value.

name: FinalRecon
description: All-in-one web reconnaissance tool (headers, whois, DNS, crawler, ports, subdomains).
bestFor: Fast web-app/domain footprinting from a single command.
trustNote: Well-known web recon tool by thewhiteh4t.
notes: Web-asset focused. Low person-finding relevance except for extracting emails/contacts off a target's website.

name: SpiderFoot
description: Automates OSINT collection across 200+ modules to map an entity's footprint.
bestFor: Automated, correlated OSINT on a name, email, username, IP, domain or phone.
trustNote: Industry-standard OSINT automation engine; open-source core, paid SpiderFoot HX cloud tier.
notes: Surfaced via this topic's related results. Open-source self-hosted version is free with a web UI; many modules need free API keys. Correlates emails, accounts, breaches, IPs and more around a seed selector - strong for building a person profile. The hosted SpiderFoot HX is paid.
